name: Pan-Roasted Rabbit with Fresh Herbs
recipeIngredient: 2 Thyme sprigs or 1/2 tsp Dried Thyme
2 Savory winter sprigs or 1/2 tsp Dried Savory
2 Rosemary sprigs or 1/4 tsp Dried Rosemary
2 Marjoram sprigs or 1/4 tsp Dried Marjoram
1 Oregano, fresh or 1/2 tsp Dried Oregano
recipeInstructions:
See recipes for Brown Chicken Stock and Roast Beef Puree.
Blanch 1 1/2 pounds of fresh spinach and reserved greens from 4 beets. Drain, rinse under cold water, squeeze out liquid, toss in a skillet over medium-high heat with 2 tablespoons butter until heated through. Season with salt.
To Marinate:
You can do this yourself or ask your butcher to cut the rabbit as follows: forelegs whole, hind legs into three pieces each (lower joint; thigh diagonally into two pieces), rib section into three pieces, loin into three pieces, neck whole. At least 2 days before serving, place the rabbit in a shallow glass or enamel pan and add marinade ingredients. Turn rabbit pieces to coat. Refrigerate, turning occasionally.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Remove rabbit from marinade. Heat about 1/4 cup olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add half of the rabbit pieces, season with salt and pepper, and sauté until golden brown (about 5 minutes). Transfer to an oven-safe casserole dish. Discard pan fat and add 1/2 cup white wine, simmering for 2 minutes before adding liquids to the casserole. Repeat with remaining rabbit pieces.
Bring chicken stock to a boil; skim if necessary, then pour into casserole (liquid should not fully cover meat). Partially cover and place in lower third of oven. After 50 minutes, check forelegs, racks, and loins for tenderness. Continue cooking until all pieces are tender (1-1.5 hours total). Transfer to plates and keep warm.
Meanwhile, blanch garlic in boiling salted water for 30 seconds, drain, then sauté in a small skillet with olive oil over high heat until golden (about 25-30 minutes). Cut into pieces and set aside.
Reduce cooking liquid over high heat until halved. Skim fat, stir arrowroot/water mixture, return to boil, add brandy, adjust seasoning, and skim again. Stir in garlic pieces and pour sauce over rabbit.
Serve with Roast Beef Puree, spinach, and beet greens.
